primarySegment.name: Fitness Enthusiasts Self-Training
primarySegment.demographics: Ages 25-45, 60% male, 40% female, income $50k-$100k+, urban/suburban areas, college-educated
primarySegment.psychographics: Self-motivated, health-conscious, value efficiency and injury prevention, comfortable with technology, prefer flexible scheduling over gym-bound training
painPoints: Fear of injury from improper form while training alone
Unable to afford $200/month for traditional personal training
Lack of confidence in exercise technique, especially for compound movements
No immediate feedback when training at home or in crowded gyms
Previous injuries or setbacks due to poor form
primarySegment.size: 12.5 million active home fitness participants in US (2026), growing 23% YoY
primarySegment.acquisitionPotential: High - actively searching for solutions, willing to pay per-use vs subscription fatigue

secondarySegment.name: Post-Rehab & Injury-Prevention Focused
secondarySegment.demographics: Ages 35-55, 55% female, 45% male, income $60k-$120k, concerned with longevity and pain-free movement
secondarySegment.psychographics: Risk-averse, previous injury history, doctor/PT recommended form checking, prioritize safety over performance, willing to invest in prevention
painPoints: Returning to exercise after injury and fearful of re-injury
Physical therapy ended but still need ongoing form guidance
Chronic pain from years of poor movement patterns
Want expert validation before progressing to heavier weights
secondarySegment.size: 8.2 million Americans in post-rehab fitness phase annually
secondarySegment.acquisitionPotential: Medium-High - longer sales cycle but higher LTV, strong word-of-mouth potential

tertiarySegment.name: Online Fitness Coaches & Trainers
tertiarySegment.demographics: Ages 25-40, certified fitness professionals, managing 10-50 remote clients, income $40k-$80k
tertiarySegment.psychographics: Entrepreneurial, tech-savvy, seeking scalable client management, value time efficiency, need to differentiate their services
painPoints: Can't provide in-person form corrections to remote clients
Time-consuming to review client videos individually
Clients get injured due to lack of real-time oversight
Need professional tools to scale coaching business
tertiarySegment.size: 150,000+ online fitness coaches in US (growing segment)
tertiarySegment.acquisitionPotential: Medium - B2B2C model, smaller market but higher volume per user

competitorAnalysis.name: Future Fitness
competitorAnalysis.model: 1-on-1 dedicated human coach with ongoing relationship
strengths: Premium positioning with dedicated coach relationship
Comprehensive program design, not just form feedback
Strong brand recognition and VC-backed growth
weaknesses: Very expensive for casual users ($2,400/year commitment)
Overkill for users who just want occasional form checks
Subscription fatigue - high churn potential
Requires ongoing monthly commitment vs pay-per-use
competitorAnalysis.differentiation: FormFit costs 90% less with pay-per-review model ($15-25/review vs $200/month), targets casual users who don't need full coaching

competitorAnalysis.name: Caliber Fitness
competitorAnalysis.model: Freemium with AI-driven workouts, human coaches for premium tiers
strengths: Robust free tier with no ads (strong acquisition tool)
Multiple pricing tiers for different needs
Strength training focus with progressive overload
Good balance of AI automation and human expertise at premium level
weaknesses: Free tier doesn't include form feedback from experts
Premium tier still expensive for occasional users
Focus on program design, form checking is secondary feature
1-on-1 coaching at $200/month same price as Future
competitorAnalysis.differentiation: FormFit focuses exclusively on form analysis expertise, not full program design - specialized solution vs generalist app

competitorAnalysis.name: Fitbod / JEFIT
competitorAnalysis.model: AI-driven workout planning with exercise libraries and form videos
strengths: Massive exercise library (1,400+ exercises with animations)
weaknesses: No personalized human form feedback - only generic videos
AI can't analyze your specific form issues
Educational content vs actionable corrections
One-size-fits-all approach to form guidance
competitorAnalysis.differentiation: FormFit provides actual certified trainer analysis of YOUR form, not generic exercise tutorials - personalized vs templated

competitorAnalysis.name: Local Personal Trainers (In-Person)
competitorAnalysis.pricing: $50-100 per session, typically sold in packages of 10-20 sessions
strengths: Real-time, in-person corrections and hands-on adjustments
weaknesses: Extremely expensive ($500-2000 for 10-session packages)
Requires gym membership and travel time
Inflexible scheduling (must coordinate calendars)
Geographic limitations
Pressure to purchase packages upfront
competitorAnalysis.differentiation: FormFit offers expert feedback at 80% lower cost, fully remote (train anywhere), submit on your schedule, pay-per-use flexibility

competitorAnalysis.name: Reddit/YouTube Free Form Checks
competitorAnalysis.model: Post videos to r/formcheck, r/fitness or YouTube for crowd-sourced advice
weaknesses: Unvetted, non-certified advice (dangerous for injury prevention)
Inconsistent quality and conflicting recommendations
No accountability or follow-up
Public posting may deter privacy-conscious users
Slow/unreliable response times
competitorAnalysis.differentiation: FormFit guarantees certified trainer review within 24-48 hours, professional liability, consistent quality, private and secure

positioning.uniqueValue: Get certified trainer feedback on your workout form for less than the cost of a single gym session. Train confidently, avoid injuries, and progress faster with expert video analysis - whenever you need it.
positioning.positioningStatement: FormFit is the affordable, flexible alternative to expensive personal training subscriptions. We serve fitness enthusiasts who want expert form validation without the $200/month commitment. Pay only for the reviews you need, when you need them.
competitiveAdvantages: Pay-per-review model (no subscriptions) - 90% cheaper than Future/Caliber premium
Specialized form analysis expertise vs generalist workout apps
Certified trainers vs anonymous Reddit advice
24-48 hour turnaround vs waiting for gym appointment
Train anywhere, anytime vs location-bound personal training

pricingStrategy.userQuestion: do we need to start for free as a starting strategy?
pricingStrategy.recommendation: Freemium Model with Low-Barrier Paid Tier
pricingStrategy.rationale:
Based on competitor analysis and direct marketing requirements, FormFit should launch with a LIMITED freemium offering, NOT a fully free product. Here's why:

1. COMPETITOR EVIDENCE: Caliber's robust free tier drives massive user acquisition (ranked #2 in acquisition performance per MobileAction data). However, they monetize through $200/month premium tiers. FormFit's pay-per-review model ($15-25/review) is already 90% cheaper than competitors, so a free tier would cannibalize revenue without the high LTV to justify it.

2. DIRECT MARKETING + PAID CHANNELS: When running Google Ads and cold email campaigns with $1,500-3,000/month budget, you're acquiring WARM leads actively searching for solutions. These users have high intent and willingness to pay. A completely free offering would waste ad spend on tire-kickers. Better to offer a low-friction paid entry ($9-15 for first review) to qualify serious buyers.

3. MARKET POSITIONING: Free alternatives exist (Reddit r/formcheck, YouTube comments). FormFit's value proposition is CERTIFIED, PROFESSIONAL, RELIABLE feedback. Starting free undermines perceived quality and attracts wrong audience segment.

4. UNIT ECONOMICS: At $15-25 per review with $5-10 trainer payout, margins are tight. CAC from Google Ads is ~$63 (fitness industry average per research). If first review is free, LTV must be 3-4 reviews minimum to break even. Freemium increases risk of 1-and-done users.

5. SUCCESSFUL PRECEDENT: Fitness apps with successful freemium (MyFitnessPal, Caliber) offer free TRACKING/PLANNING but charge for EXPERT ACCESS. FormFit's core product IS expert access, so free tier must be limited.

RECOMMENDATION: Offer '1 Free Form Check' (limited to one exercise, text feedback only) to reduce barrier, then $15/review for standard service or $99/month for unlimited reviews (for power users). This balances acquisition with monetization.

freeTier.name: Try FormFit Free
freeTier.limitations: ONE-TIME ONLY: Submit 1 video (max 1 minute, single exercise), receive text-only feedback from certified trainer within 72 hours. No video markup, no follow-up questions.
freeTier.purpose: Lead magnet for Google Ads, cold email campaigns. Qualifies intent, builds trust, converts to paid.
freeTier.conversionTarget: 25-35% convert to paid after free trial (industry benchmark for fitness freemium)

paidTiers.name: Pay-Per-Review
features: Submit video up to 5 minutes (multiple exercises)
Video markup with timestamps showing form issues
One round of follow-up questions included
paidTiers.targetSegment: Primary - Casual users needing occasional validation (1-2 reviews/month)

paidTiers.name: Unlimited Monthly
features: UNLIMITED form reviews (submit as many as needed)
Dedicated trainer matching (same trainer reviews your videos for consistency)
paidTiers.targetSegment: Power users, online coaches managing clients, post-rehab users needing frequent validation

paidTiers.name: Coaching Teams (B2B)
features: White-label form review service for online coaches
Coach dashboard to manage client videos

expectedConversionRates.freeToFirstPaid: 25-35% (benchmark: fitness freemium apps convert 20-40% per research)
expectedConversionRates.upgradeToUnlimited: 8-12% of pay-per-review users upgrade after 3+ reviews

pricingPsychology.anchoringStrategy: Display Unlimited Monthly ($99) prominently to make Pay-Per-Review ($15-25) feel like a bargain for casual users
pricingPsychology.urgencyTactics: First review 50% off for new users (creates $7.50-12.50 entry point, better than free for qualifying intent)
pricingPsychology.bundleOptions: 3-pack of reviews for $40 (save $5-15), 10-pack for $120 (save $30-50) - encourages bulk purchase

selectedChannels.name: Google Ads (Search + YouTube)
selectedChannels.whySelected: HIGHEST INTENT CHANNEL for direct marketing. Users searching 'form check personal trainer', 'improve squat form', 'deadlift technique help' have IMMEDIATE NEED and willingness to pay. Health/Fitness has strong 4.5x ROAS with $5 avg CPC and 6.8% conversion rate (per 2026 benchmarks). YouTube pre-roll targets fitness content viewers with visual demonstration of FormFit value prop. 95% automatable with Google's Smart Bidding.
selectedChannels.setupTime: 12-15 hours (keyword research, ad creation, landing page optimization, conversion tracking)

campaigns.adCopy: Get Expert Form Feedback in 48hrs | Certified Trainers | First Review $12.50 | Avoid Injuries, Lift Confidently
Stop Training in Pain | Expert Form Analysis Finds Your Issues | Certified Trainers | 24-48hr Turnaround

campaigns.targeting: Viewers of AthleanX, Jeff Nippard, Squat University, form-focused fitness content
campaigns.adFormat: 15-second skippable - Show split screen of bad form → trainer markup → corrected form with CTA

automationSetup: Performance Max campaigns for YouTube (AI-driven placement)

selectedChannels.name: Retargeting Ads (Google Display + Meta)
selectedChannels.whySelected: ESSENTIAL for direct marketing conversion. Only 2-5% convert on first visit. Retargeting captures the 95% who researched but didn't buy. Fitness industry has HIGH consideration time (users compare options, read reviews). Retargeting brings them back at 70% lower CPC than cold traffic. Fully automated with dynamic ads showing abandoned cart/review reminder.
selectedChannels.setupTime: 8-10 hours (pixel installation, audience creation, creative design)

campaigns.type: Visited Landing Page - Didn't Sign Up
campaigns.message: Still have questions? See real FormFit reviews. First analysis just $12.50.
Complete your first form check. Upload takes 2 minutes. 50% off today.
Loved your first review? Get unlimited for $99/month. Save vs pay-per-review.

automationSetup: Automated audience list building (Google Analytics 4 integration)
Frequency capping to avoid ad fatigue
Sequential messaging based on funnel stage

selectedChannels.name: Cold Email Outreach (Niche Communities)
selectedChannels.whySelected: HIGHLY TARGETED outreach to fitness enthusiasts in niche communities (CrossFit gyms, powerlifting clubs, online coaching groups). Unlike generic cold email, this targets KNOWN fitness participants via scraped emails from gym websites, Meetup groups, Strava clubs. Personalization at scale with Lemlist. Low cost ($0.10 per email) with 1-5% conversion rate. 85% automated after initial list building + template creation.
selectedChannels.setupTime: 15-20 hours (audience research, list building, email sequence writing, setup in Lemlist)
tools: Apollo.io or UpLead ($100/month for lead data)

targetLists.size: ~50,000 reachable via gym websites, Facebook groups
targetLists.personalization: Mention their specific gym, reference CrossFit movements (clean, snatch), pain points (mobility, injury prevention)
Reference home training challenges, lack of in-person coaching, equipment they likely own (seen in photos)

targetLists.size: ~30,000 via physical therapy clinic communities, injury recovery groups
targetLists.personalization: Emphasize safety, injury prevention, professional validation before progressing

emailSequence.subject: [Name], training alone without form feedback is risky
emailSequence.body: Hey [Name], saw you're part of [Gym/Community]. Quick question - when you're hitting heavy [exercise they likely do], who's checking your form? Most people training at [home/their gym] don't have access to real-time coaching. One bad rep can mean weeks of setback. What if you could get a certified trainer to analyze your form for less than a single gym session? Reply if you're interested in how FormFit works.

emailSequence.subject: How [Similar User] fixed their [common issue]
emailSequence.body: Just helped a [CrossFitter/home gym user/runner] fix their [squat/deadlift] form that was causing [knee/back] pain. Before/after video attached. They paid $15 for the review. Avoided months of potential injury. Want to see how it works for your lifts?

emailSequence.subject: 50% off your first form check (expires Friday)
emailSequence.body: [Name], final follow-up. This week only: First form review for $7.50 (normally $15). Upload your video, get expert feedback in 48 hours. No subscription, pay once. Spots limited to 50 reviews this week. Claim yours: [link]

automationSetup: Lemlist automated sequence (sends emails on schedule, tracks opens/clicks)
Automated follow-ups based on behavior (clicked but didn't convert → retarget via email)
Email validation to maintain sender reputation
Weekly list refresh (new members join gyms/communities constantly)
selectedChannels.ongoingMaintenance: 2-3 hours/week (review responses, update email copy based on performance, refresh targeting lists)

selectedChannels.name: Affiliate/Partnership Program
selectedChannels.whySelected: LEVERAGE EXISTING AUDIENCES of fitness influencers, bloggers, and online coaches. Partners promote FormFit to their followers for 20-30% commission per sale. Zero upfront cost (pay only for performance). Scales infinitely once set up. Partners create content (reviews, tutorials) that ranks in Google, providing passive organic traffic. Takes 4-6 weeks to recruit partners but then runs on autopilot.
selectedChannels.monthlyBudget: $200-300 for affiliate software + outreach to recruit partners
selectedChannels.setupTime: 10-12 hours (set up Rewardful/PartnerStack, create partner resources, outreach to potential affiliates)

partnerTypes.commission: 30% per sale ($4.50 per $15 review, $30 per $99/month subscription)
partnerTypes.recruitment: DM on Instagram/YouTube - fitness coaches, injury prevention specialists, physical therapists
partnerTypes.content: Instagram Stories showing their own form review, YouTube video 'I tried FormFit', blog post review

partnerTypes.commission: 25% per sale + $50 flat fee for in-depth review post
partnerTypes.recruitment: Email outreach to blogs ranking for 'best personal training apps', 'online fitness coaching reviews'
partnerTypes.content: SEO-optimized review posts that rank for '[competitor] alternative', 'affordable form coaching'

partnerTypes.commission: 20% recurring on B2B plan ($60/month per coach they refer)
partnerTypes.recruitment: LinkedIn outreach, fitness coaching Facebook groups
partnerTypes.content: Recommend FormFit to their clients as form-checking tool, white-label integration

automationSetup: Rewardful auto-tracks referrals via unique links, calculates commissions
Automated monthly payouts via PayPal/bank transfer
Partner dashboard shows real-time earnings, conversion data
Automated onboarding email sequence (welcome, best practices, creative assets)
selectedChannels.ongoingMaintenance: 3-4 hours/week (recruit new partners, send performance updates, create new promotional assets, answer partner questions)

rejectedChannels.name: LinkedIn Ads
rejectedChannels.whyRejected: Too expensive for B2C fitness market. LinkedIn CPC averages $8-15 (vs Google $5) and is optimized for B2B/professional audiences. FormFit's primary segment (fitness enthusiasts) doesn't actively browse LinkedIn for fitness solutions. Even for tertiary B2B segment (online coaches), organic outreach + partnerships are more cost-effective than LinkedIn's high CPMs. Budget better allocated to Google/YouTube where fitness intent is higher.

rejectedChannels.name: TikTok/Instagram Organic Content
rejectedChannels.whyRejected: Violates 'Fully Automated' requirement. Organic social media requires daily content creation (filming, editing, posting), community management (responding to comments/DMs), and trend-chasing. Even with batching, requires 10-15 hours/week ongoing. Not automatable. While effective for brand building, doesn't align with user's 'zero daily maintenance' constraint. Better to use PAID social (retargeting) which is automatable, or leverage affiliate influencers who create content on FormFit's behalf.

rejectedChannels.name: SEO/Content Marketing (Blog)
rejectedChannels.whyRejected: TOO SLOW for 2-4 week timeline requirement. SEO takes 3-6 months to see results, requires ongoing content production (8-12 blog posts/month), technical optimization, and link building. Not automatable without expensive content agencies ($3,000-5,000/month). User's budget ($1,500-3,000/month) and timeline (need results in 2-4 weeks) make SEO non-viable for initial strategy. Recommend revisiting in Month 4+ once paid channels prove unit economics.

rejectedChannels.name: Podcast Sponsorships
rejectedChannels.whyRejected: High minimum commitments ($500-2,000 per podcast per month for 8-12 week runs) with unproven attribution. Fitness podcasts require 2-3 month commitments, making it risky for unvalidated product. CPM is $18-25 (vs Google Ads' performance-based CPC). Can't A/B test or optimize mid-flight. Better to allocate budget to Google Ads where you can iterate daily, pause underperformers, and scale winners. Revisit podcasts once CAC/LTV is proven via direct response channels.

rejectedChannels.name: App Store / Google Play Listing
rejectedChannels.whyRejected: FormFit is WEB-BASED (Next.js), not a native mobile app. Building iOS/Android apps would require $50k-150k development cost plus $30k-50k/year maintenance (per fitness app development research). Massive distraction from MVP validation. App stores also have 15-30% fees on transactions, reducing already-tight margins on $15-25 reviews. Web app allows faster iteration, cross-platform access, and avoids app store approval delays. If FormFit proves product-market fit, consider native apps in Year 2.

rejectedChannels.name: Marketplace Listings (Capterra, G2)
rejectedChannels.whyRejected: B2B SaaS marketplaces optimized for business software buyers researching enterprise tools. FormFit is B2C/prosumer. Capterra listing fees are $300-500/month with low traffic for fitness category. G2 requires 10+ reviews before prominent placement. Fitness consumers don't browse Capterra for personal training solutions - they use Google Search, YouTube, Instagram. Budget better spent on Google Ads reaching high-intent consumers. Potentially revisit for B2B tier (coaching teams) once that segment scales.

rejectedChannels.name: Facebook Ads (Cold Traffic)
rejectedChannels.whyRejected: While automatable, Facebook cold traffic for fitness has MUCH higher CAC ($80-120) than Google Search ($45-65) because users are in 'scroll mode', not 'search mode'. Fitness ads also face saturation - 67% of fitness businesses use Facebook Ads, creating auction competition. Facebook's audience targeting has degraded post-iOS14 privacy changes. Better to use Facebook/Meta ONLY for retargeting (included in strategy) where it excels, not cold acquisition. Google Search captures higher intent at lower cost.

No fitness industry gatekeeping - everyone deserves expert guidance, regardless of experience level.

secondarySegment.emotionalHook: Don't let fear of re-injury keep you from the activities you love.

tools.name: Google Ads
tools.purpose: Primary acquisition channel - Search and YouTube campaigns with Smart Bidding automation

tools.name: Meta Ads Manager
tools.cost: $0/month (platform fee), ad spend is $300/month for retargeting
tools.purpose: Retargeting campaigns to convert abandoning visitors

tools.name: Google Analytics 4
tools.purpose: Conversion tracking, attribution, automated audience building for retargeting

tools.name: Google Tag Manager
tools.purpose: Tracking pixel management for ads and analytics, no-code updates

tools.name: Lemlist
tools.purpose: Cold email automation - sequences, personalization, deliverability optimization

tools.name: Apollo.io or UpLead
tools.purpose: Lead data for cold email campaigns (emails of fitness gym members, coaches, enthusiasts)

tools.name: Rewardful
tools.purpose: Affiliate program management - tracking, commission calculation, automated payouts

oneTimeSetup: Install Google Analytics 4 and configure conversion tracking (2 hours)
Set up Google Tag Manager with tracking pixels for Ads, Meta (2 hours)
Create Google Ads account structure: 3 campaigns, 12 ad groups, 40 keywords (6 hours)
Write 20 responsive search ads + 5 YouTube video scripts (4 hours)
Design landing pages in Unbounce: 3 variants for A/B testing (6 hours)
Set up Meta retargeting campaigns with dynamic ads (3 hours)
Configure Lemlist: email sequences (3 templates), personalization variables, sending schedule (4 hours)
Build initial cold email list: scrape 5,000 contacts from target communities (6 hours)
Set up Rewardful affiliate program: commission structure, partner resources, onboarding flow (4 hours)
Create affiliate marketing assets: banner ads, email templates, social posts (3 hours)
Recruit initial 5-10 affiliates via outreach (8 hours)
Configure Zapier automations: new user → CRM, failed payment → email, etc. (2 hours)
Set up automated reporting dashboard (Google Data Studio) (3 hours)
automationSetup.setupTimeEstimate: 56 hours total (can be completed in 7-10 days working 6-8 hours/day, or outsourced to marketing agency for $3,000-5,000)

maintenanceBreakdown.task: Review Google Ads performance, pause low performers, adjust bids
Review cold email replies, move interested leads to sales flow
Recruit new affiliate partners, send performance updates
Refresh cold email lists with new contacts from communities
Review analytics dashboard, update stakeholder report

automationSetup.maintenanceNote: Total 6.25 hours/week = 90 minutes/day. This is MINIMAL maintenance compared to content marketing (20+ hours/week) or organic social (15+ hours/week). All heavy lifting is automated. Manual work is high-leverage: reviewing data, making optimization decisions, relationship management with affiliates.

successMetrics.ltvCalculation: Mix of pay-per-review users ($45-60 LTV) and subscription users ($1,188 LTV). Assume 85% pay-per-review, 15% subscription: (0.85 × $52.50) + (0.15 × $1,188) = $223 blended LTV
successMetrics.ltvToCacRatio: 3.4:1 to 3.7:1 (exceeds 3:1 benchmark for healthy unit economics)

conversionRateBenchmarks.signupToFirstPurchase: 40-50% (with free tier or 50% off first review)

month1.notes: Negative ROI expected - testing and optimizing

kpiDashboard.measurement: Total spend per channel ÷ new customers from channel
Total marketing spend ÷ total new customers
Average revenue per customer over 12 months (cohort analysis)
Landing page visitors who complete first purchase ÷ total visitors
Customers who make 2nd purchase ÷ total first-time customers
Revenue attributed to Google Ads ÷ Google Ads spend
Email recipients who purchase ÷ total emails sent
Affiliate link clicks → purchases ÷ total affiliate clicks

week1: Set up Google Analytics 4, Tag Manager, conversion tracking
Design 3 landing page variants in Unbounce
week2: Configure Lemlist and write email sequences
Build initial cold email list (5,000 contacts)

risk1.mitigation: Set hard CPA caps in Google Ads at $60. Pause campaigns if CAC > $70 for 2 consecutive weeks. Shift budget to lower-CAC channels (email, affiliates).
risk1.fallback: Pivot to B2B model (coaching teams at $299/month) with higher LTV to support higher CAC

risk2.risk: Low conversion rate (<3%) on landing pages
risk2.mitigation: A/B test 3 landing page variants with different value props, social proof, pricing displays. Use heatmaps (Hotjar) to identify friction points. Test 50% off first review vs free tier lead magnet.
risk2.fallback: Offer 7-day money-back guarantee to reduce purchase anxiety

risk3.mitigation: Use email validation (ZeroBounce) before sending. Warm up sending domain for 2 weeks. Keep send volume under 100/day initially. Monitor bounce rate (<2%) and spam complaint rate (<0.1%).
risk3.fallback: Switch to LinkedIn outreach if email deliverability fails (more manual, but guaranteed inbox placement)

risk4.risk: Affiliate partners don't drive meaningful conversions
risk4.mitigation: Provide partners with high-converting assets (email templates, social posts, before/after examples). Offer performance bonuses ($100 for first 10 referrals). Only invest time in partners who drive 2+ conversions in first month.
risk4.fallback: Redirect affiliate budget to Google Ads if partners underperform after 60 days

risk5.mitigation: Build brand loyalty early with exceptional service. Lock in customers with 10-review prepay discounts. Expand to adjacent services (nutrition, mobility coaching) that competitors don't offer.
risk5.fallback: Pivot to B2B2C (white-label for gyms/coaches) where FormFit becomes infrastructure, not consumer brand

immediate: Hire marketing contractor or agency for setup (56 hours, $3,000-5,000) OR allocate internal team time
Order list of tasks from executionTimeline and begin Week 1 setup

Create Google Ads account and first 3 campaigns
Design landing pages with clear CTA and social proof
Launch ads with daily budget monitoring
nextSteps.firstMonthGoal: Acquire 30-50 new customers, validate CAC is <$65, test messaging variants, identify top-performing channel
